summary: Surah Az-Zukhruf emphasizes the wisdom and clarity of the Qur’an, rejects false beliefs about Allah having offspring, and refutes the worship of idols and angels. It recounts the stories of previous prophets, including Ibrahim and Musa, and highlights the arrogance and stubbornness of disbelievers. The surah contrasts the fleeting nature of worldly adornments with the eternal reward of the Hereafter for the pious, and warns of the fate awaiting those who reject the truth.
